name: Shimōsa
description: A prosperous province centred on the Tone River. Its fertile plains yield abundant rice, while its coast provides fish and salt.

name: Kogane
description: A bustling river port on the Tone River, the heart of Shimōsa's trade.

name: Sakura
description: An ancient castle town perched on a wooded hill. The Chiba clan's fortress overlooks the plains.

name: Chōshi
description: A fishing village at the mouth of the Tone River.

name: Narita
description: A quiet village centred around a Shingon temple. Pilgrims pass through.

name: Yachimata
description: A small farming hamlet surrounded by vast millet fields.

name: Kogane Woods
description: Dense mixed forest of oak, maple, and pine. Deer and wild boar roam.

name: Tone Riverside
description: The broad Tone River. Herons stand in the shallows.

name: Shimōsa Coast
description: Sandy beaches stretching along the eastern sea.

name: Hitachi
description: A rugged province of rocky coasts, deep forests, and iron‑rich mountains. The Satake clan rules from Mito.

name: Mito
description: Seat of the Satake clan. A formidable castle overlooks the eastern sea.

name: Kasama
description: A market town known for stone quarries and an Inari shrine.

name: Hitachiōta
description: A remote mountain village of charcoal burners and hunters.

name: Nakaminato
description: A small fishing port with a natural harbour.

name: Hitachi Forest
description: Deep woods of ancient cedar and cypress.

name: Hitachi Mountains
description: Rugged peaks with visible iron ore veins.

name: Hitachi Coast
description: Rocky shoreline with crashing waves.

name: Shimotsuke
description: A land of rolling hills and broad plains, famous for its horses. The Utsunomiya clan controls the province.

name: Utsunomiya
description: The provincial seat, famous for its horse markets.

name: Nikkō
description: A sacred mountain village at the foot of the holy peaks.

name: Mooka
description: An agricultural village on the fertile plain. Barley and soybeans grow in abundance.

name: Utsunomiya Highlands
description: Rolling hills covered in susuki grass. Wild horses can be spotted.

name: Nikkō Mountains
description: Steep, sacred peaks. Waterfalls plunge into moss‑covered gorges.

name: Kōzuke
description: A province of wide plains and high mountains, contested by the Uesugi and Nitta remnants.

name: Takasaki
description: A trade hub on the Usui River.

name: Maebashi
description: A growing town on the Tone River. Rice paddies stretch to the horizon.

name: Numata
description: A remote highland village perched on a mountain pass.

name: Tone Riverbank
description: The broad river winds through the plain, its waters cold and swift.

name: Mikuni Mountains
description: The pass to Echigo. Snow lingers here even in spring.

name: Musashi
description: The vast Kantō plain's heartland. Edo, a growing port town, lies on its southern shore.

name: Edo
description: A bustling port town on the Sumida River. The fish market is renowned.

name: Kawagoe
description: A castle town built on the great plain, known for its sturdy fortifications.

name: Hachiōji
description: A stopping point for travelers heading west toward Kai.

name: Arakawa
description: A fishing and farming village along the Arakawa River. Ferries cross here.

name: Musashi Plain
description: The vast Kantō plain stretches endlessly.

name: Sumida River
description: A wide, slow river teeming with fish.

name: Musashi Coast
description: The shore of Edo Bay. On clear days, Mount Fuji can be seen.

name: Kazusa
description: A coastal province of gentle hills and sheltered coves, famed for its shellfish.

name: Ichinomiya
description: A prosperous coastal town known for its shellfish and the great shrine to Awa-no-kami.

name: Mobara
description: An inland village surrounded by wooded hills. Known for sweet plums.

name: Katsuura
description: A deep‑water fishing port where bonito and tuna are brought in daily.

name: Kazusa Hills
description: Gentle hills covered in bamboo groves.

name: Kazusa Coast
description: Dramatic cliffs and small coves. Sea hawks nest in the rocks.

name: Awa
description: The tip of the Bōsō Peninsula, where the mountains meet the open ocean.

name: Tateyama
description: The peninsula's end, dominated by the great sea shrine.

name: Shirahama
description: A white‑sand beach village where fishermen brave the open ocean.

name: Awa Cliffs
description: Sheer cliffs drop to churning waters.

name: Awa Mountains
description: Forested peaks at the peninsula's spine. Isolated and wild.

name: Sagami
description: A province of deep history and natural beauty. Kamakura, the ancient shogunal capital, still holds the Great Buddha.

name: Kamakura
description: The ancient seat of the Minamoto shogunate. Temples and warrior houses cling to the hills.

name: Odawara
description: A strategic castle town guarding the Tōkaidō road.

name: Hakone
description: A mountain village near the great lake. Hot springs steam among the volcanic rocks.

name: Sagami Bay
description: The blue bay stretches wide. Fuji can be seen on clear days.

name: Hakone Mountains
description: Volcanic peaks and hot springs. The old Tōkaidō road winds through the pass.
